publication/title: Vision transformers: The next frontier for deep learning-based ophthalmic image analysis.
publication/authors: Wu JH, Koseoglu ND, Jones C, Liu TYA.
publication/journal: Saudi journal of ophthalmology : official journal of the Saudi Ophthalmological Society
publication/tags:
* Vision Transformers
* Ophthalmic Image Analysis
* Diabetic Retinopathy
* Glaucoma Detection
* Optical Coherence Tomography
* Color Fundus Photographs
* Hybrid Models
dataset/provenance:
* **Data Source Name**:
  * Kaggle dataset
  * Messidor-1 dataset
  * EyePACS dataset
  * DDR dataset
  * Ocular Hypertension Treatment Study
  * SIGF dataset
  * Multiple publicly available fundus datasets
  * OCT-angiography images
  * Messidor dataset

* **Total Data Points**:
  * Not enough information is available

* **Previous Use**:
  * Kaggle dataset: Yes. Wu et al. used this dataset to train a ViTs-based model for multi-class grading of DR severity.
  * Messidor-1 dataset: Yes. Sun et al. used this dataset to classify referable DR.
  * EyePACS dataset: Yes. Sun et al. used this dataset to classify DR into 5 grades.
  * DDR dataset: Yes. Gu et al. used this dataset for multi-class DR grading.
  * Ocular Hypertension Treatment Study: Yes. Fan et al. used this dataset to train the DeiT model for glaucoma detection.
  * SIGF dataset: Yes. Hu et al. used this dataset to predict the time to glaucoma onset.
  * Multiple publicly available fundus datasets: Yes. Mallick et al. used these datasets to detect glaucoma.
  * OCT-angiography images: Yes. Kihara et al. used these images as ground truth for detecting nonexudative macular neovascularization.
  * Messidor dataset: Yes. Yao et al. used this dataset to detect and perform multi-class grading of diabetic macular edema.

model/interpretability:
* **Model Interpretability**: Black-box.
  * The models discussed in the text are primarily deep learning models, specifically Vision Transformers (ViTs) and Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs). These models are known for their complexity and are generally considered black-box models, meaning their internal workings are not easily interpretable.
  * The text mentions that DeiT (Data-efficient image Transformer) highlighted localized areas of the neuroretinal rim, while ResNet50 demonstrated diffuse highlighting around the optic disc. This suggests that some level of interpretability was achieved through post-hoc analysis, but the models themselves are not inherently interpretable.

* **XAI Methods**: None.
  * The text does not explicitly mention the use of specific Explainable AI (XAI) methods. The interpretability discussed is based on the models' attention mechanisms and post-hoc analysis of highlighted regions, but no formal XAI techniques are named.

evaluation/measure:
**Performance Metrics:**

* **Diabetic Retinopathy (DR) Grading:**
  * Accuracy: 0.82–0.87 (stand-alone transformers), 0.95 (ensemble model), 0.83–0.84 (CoT-XNet), 0.71–0.83 (other CNNs and DL models)
  * AUC: 0.98–0.99 (lesion-aware transformer on Messidor-1), 0.89–0.97 (prior SOTA DL approaches), 0.88 (lesion-aware transformer on EyePACS), 0.84–0.87 (other CNNs and DL approaches)
  * Kappa: 0.88 (lesion-aware transformer on EyePACS), 0.84–0.87 (other CNNs and DL approaches)

* **Glaucoma Detection:**
  * AUC: 0.97 (ViTs), 0.95 (ConvNext), 0.83–0.92 (prior CNNs), 0.82–0.86 (DeiT), 0.83–0.89 (ResNet50), 0.98 (ensemble ViTs-based models), 0.94 (ViTs-based models for predicting time to glaucoma onset)
  * Sensitivity: 0.92 (ensemble ViTs-based models), 0.88 (ViTs-based models for predicting time to glaucoma onset)
  * Specificity: 0.97 (ensemble ViTs-based models), 0.94 (ViTs-based models for predicting time to glaucoma onset)

* **Age-Related Macular Degeneration (AMD):**
  * DICE score: 0.457 (Swin transformer-based model), 0.508 (UNet variant)
  * AUC: 0.82 (ViTs for detecting neovascular AMD)
  * Sensitivity: 0.90 (ViTs for detecting neovascular AMD)
  * Specificity: 0.91 (ViTs for detecting neovascular AMD)

* **Multi-Disease/Lesion Classification:**
  * Accuracy: 0.996 (ViTs), 0.340–0.985 (CNNs), 0.99 (hybrid model), 0.92–0.94 (stand-alone CNNs), 0.95–0.99 (stand-alone transformers), 0.86–0.92 (hybrid ConvNet-Transformer network), 0.76–0.90 (stand-alone CNNs), 0.79–0.86 (stand-alone ViT models), 0.977 (hybrid model), 0.741–0.901 (other models)
  * Sensitivity: 0.977 (hybrid model), 0.653–0.868 (other models)
  * Specificity: 0.992 (hybrid model), 0.718–0.900 (other models)

* **Other Ophthalmic Diseases and Retinal Lesions:**
  * AUC: 0.99 (ViT-CNN hybrid model for vessel segmentation)
  * Mean Average Precision: 0.86 (detection transformer for pathologic lesions)
  * Accuracy: 0.90–0.91 (ViT-based model and deep CNN model for detecting posterior vitreous detachment)
evaluation/comparison:
* **Comparison Performed**: Yes
  * **List of tools/baselines**:
    * **Diabetic Retinopathy (DR) Grading**:
      * **Stand-alone Transformers**: Accuracy: 0.82–0.87
      * **Hybrid Models**:
        * **Lesion-aware Transformer**: Combining Vision Transformers (ViTs) and ResNet50
        * **CoT-XNet**: Combining Contextual Transformer and Xception (CNN)
      * **Other SOTA DL Approaches**:
        * **Xception**
        * **DeepMT-DR**
        * **CABNet**
    * **Glaucoma Detection**:
